title: Failure to set up network for podman-compose | [ELK] Compose
body:
### Issue Description

# SPECS

> podman version                                                                                                                                                                                                    
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.6.0
API Version:  4.6.0
Go Version:   go1.20.6
Git Commit:   38e6fab9664c6e59b66e73523b307a56130316ae-dirty
Built:        Fri Jul 21 14:38:28 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

> podman-compose version                                                                                                                                                                                                    
podman-compose version: 1.0.6
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.6.0
podman-compose version 1.0.6
podman --version 
podman version 4.6.0
exit code: 0


----

# Issue

When trying to run a Compose stack, I get an error

The source is here: https://github.com/elkninja/elastic-stack-docker-part-one/

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Clone the mentioned repo
2. Try to `podman-compose -f $file up`
3. 


### Describe the results you received

```

exit code: 125
```

### Describe the results you expected

The storage works.

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @DistantThunder.

> WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network elastic not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network elastic from disk 
Error: unable to start container 734119a77696e1612f95f1cd14558c9f1daa56aee434e86e0a99a2ae5f839b8c: plugin type="tuning" failed (add): cni plugin tuning failed: open /etc/cni/tuning/allowlist.conf: permission denied
exit code: 125

Did you investigate this error?

Is there are a specific reason why you're using the CNI backend?  There's a new network since Podman 4.0 (see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-new-network-stack).

FWIW, I don't think this compose file will properly work with Podman as it does things like mounting `/var/lib/docker/containers`.  This requires a) to run as root (so rootless Podman won't work) and b) expects Docker to be executing the workload.

As @vrothberg mentioned we are recommending netavark, it will work with that.
The easiest way to switch is to make sure `netavark` and `aardvark-dns` are installed then run `podman system reset`. You can confirm the backend with `podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}`.

---
As for the actual error it looks like the plugins added a new file which rootless users have no access to: https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/693

This is pretty bad for us as this will make CNI plugins >= v1.2 incompatible with rootless podman. 
There might be ways for us to work around that but I really don't think it is worth out time as out focus is on netavark and CNI is deprecated anyway.

As for an actual workaround you should be able to create the `/etc/cni/tuning` directory and make it and the parent readable for all users.

Hello, @vrothberg  @Luap99 , thank you for your quick answers here.

I had no idea I was using a specific "cni" network plugin that wasn't the recommended default. I usually run my containers rootless so no idea this could be an issue.

> Did you investigate this error?

Is there are a specific reason why you're using the CNI backend? There's a new network since Podman 4.0 (see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-new-network-stack).

I could see it's a permission + potentially missing dir issue but I had no interest in pursuing that since the goal is to run rootless and I have been able to do that until now.




> The easiest way to switch is to make sure netavark and aardvark-dns are installed then run podman system reset. You can confirm the backend with podman info --format {{.Host.NetworkBackend}}.


I went with this and it appears I'm using "netavark" now. I have also removed the docker dir mounting.

Now I encountered various other problems, namely this section:

> 108     ulimits:
109       memlock:
110         soft: -1
111         hard: -1

Figuring out what may be the best option here.
i've run into the same issue with the ports not working after upgrading podman-compose, nothing seems to fix it.

So I could make this work by:

* Wiping my environment with the full reset command: `podman system reset`.
* And then I simply removed the ulimits block. As these cannot exceed the system's and will need to be necessarily lower or perhaps matching. I had a satisfying value system-wide so I was wine just removing the block.
* Lastly ofc, also remove the docker resources mounting

After doing these three steps, I was able to get the stack up.

> i've run into the same issue with the ports not working after upgrading podman-compose, nothing seems to fix it.

I don't see how this is related. If you have reproducer and checked with the latest version please fill a new issue with all requested information.

---

As far as this CNI problem is concerned I will close this issue as wontfix given there is a workaround. We strongly recommend users to migrate to netavark, CNI is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
